titlestmt.titleproper: Chicago, Ill. Grand Avenue Congregational Church records, 1911-1929.
titlestmt.author: Flint McColgan

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 29, 2026 at 17:08

did.unittitle: Chicago, Ill. Grand Avenue Congregational Church records, 1911-1929.
did.abstract: The Grand Avenue Congregational Church began as the Central Sunday School at Grand and Lawndale avenues in Chicago circa 1890. On March 6, 1929, the church voted to absorb the Pacific Congregational Church. The church merged with fellow Chicago church Pilgrim German Congregational Church to form the United Congregational Church in 1943, which itself closed in 1976. Included in this collection are meeting minutes, listings of church officers, a brief church history, and membership records.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to this collection is unrestricted and open to the public.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use
userestrict.p: Items in this collection are subject to U.S. Copyright Law. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine the copyright status of collection items and to secure any permissions necessary for their reproduction and publication. Requests for permission to publish material must be discussed with the archivist or librarian.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], in the Chicago, Ill. Grand Avenue Congregational Church records, 1911-1929, RG5262. The Congregational Library & Archives, Boston, MA.

acqinfo.head: Acquisition Information
acqinfo.p: Materials were donated to the Congregational Library & Archives by by the Chicago Theological Seminary in April 2014; accession number 2014-109.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection was processed by Flint McColgan, April 2026, according to the rules of DACS Second Edition.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Grand Avenue Congregational Church began as the Central Sunday School at Grand and Lawndale avenues in Chicago circa 1890. It began holding Sunday services above a feed store on that same corner in September 1892, under the leadership of Chicago Congregational Theological Seminary student J. U. Stotts. In 1894, the church rented and rehabilitated an abandoned store building nearby. The church would obtain a permanent location in the next decade when it purchased a lot in 1907 and built a building there. The church dedicated the completed building on June 4, 1911. The church, experiencing significant growth, constructed a new addition called the Church House beginning in 1923. On March 6, 1929, the church voted to absorb the Pacific Congregational Church. The church merged with fellow Chicago church Pilgrim German Congregational Church to form the United Congregational Church in 1943. The United Congregational Church closed in 1976.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These church records consist of a single volume and six loose pages which include meeting minutes, listings of church officers, a brief church history, and membership records.
